title: EPFO interest credit begins: How members can check if the amount is credited to their accounts
summary: EPFO members can see their annual interest of 8.25% for FY2025-26 in their accounts starting July 15. Here's how you can check your PF balance online.

title: Property registry alone doesn't prove ownership: Here's what else homebuyers need to do
summary: Purchasing and registering a property does not guarantee legal ownership. Validity relies on the entire title chain and documentation. Mutation, while important for records, does not affect ownership rights, experts said.&nbsp;

title: Trump's accounts sold Micron, bought Nvidia: Expert warns, ‘Don't mistake it for a strategy’
summary: Donald Trump's investment accounts, managed by third parties, have executed over 6,100 stock trades in 2023. Notably, they sold Micron shares while acquiring Nvidia stock, highlighting a contradiction between Trump's public praise for Micron and his accounts' trading decisions.

title: EPFO alert: Why updating your profile photo is crucial for e-nominations and PF claims

title: Gifting stocks to your spouse, child or friend? Check how the tax rules differ in three scenarios
summary: Gifting stocks can trigger tax implications depending on the relationship between the donor and recipient. Transfers to close relatives are often tax-exempt, while gifts to others exceeding ₹50,000 are taxable.&nbsp;

title: How can families preserve art, jewellery and heirlooms for future generations?
summary: A trust can help families preserve art, jewellery and heirlooms across generations by setting clear rules on ownership, access, conservation, display, monetization and succession.

title: Monsoon is here: Why you're paying out of pocket for sudden illnesses despite having health insurance
summary: Health insurance covers major hospitalisation costs, but many medical expenses, such as doctor consultations and medicines, are often paid out of pocket. Experts say financial preparedness and understanding policy coverage can help families avoid unexpected healthcare costs.

title: SIP returns disappointing? Here's when to stay invested and when to switch funds
summary: Negative or muted SIP returns in the initial years are not uncommon, especially for equity mutual funds. Experts say investors should judge a fund over a full market cycle and switch only if there are signs of structural issues.

title: NRI tax filing: Moved abroad? Here's when you still need to file an ITR in India
summary: Moving abroad does not end your tax obligations in India. From excess TDS and capital losses to property sales and future tax planning, here's when NRIs should file an ITR.
